mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, consumer spending collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real sales of autos and light trucks combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without monetary erosion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when CPIAUCSL monthly change exceeds 0.5%, or interaction term Rank IC remains below 0.04 over 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery periods, a monthly increase in the index of industrial production signals capacity utilization improvements and margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in post-shock recovery windows, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day forward returns across all market conditions, with Rank IC below 0.01 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in housing starts signals a collapsing construction sector and reduced consumer confidence, dragging down S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 60% of periods following a monthly drop in HOUST exceeding 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic strength, reducing recession risk premiums and lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a quarterly decline in real GDP signals demand exhaustion and impending corporate earnings contraction, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 declines fail to predict negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in late-cycle regimes, with directional accuracy below 45% over a 10-year out-of-sample window.
